title: ABSS-Net v1.0.0 - Reliable and calibrated diabetic retinopathy grading from Gaussian-filtered fundus images using attention and gated state-space feature mixing
description:
Archived source code and derived research outputs supporting the article Reliable and calibrated diabetic retinopathy grading from Gaussian-filtered fundus images using attention and gated state-space feature mixing.

ABSS-Net is a compact (5,470,756-parameter) network for five-class ICDR diabetic retinopathy grading on Gaussian-filtered colour fundus images. It combines an ImageNet/Noisy-Student EfficientNet-B0 multi-scale feature pyramid, Convolutional Block Attention Modules at three feature scales, two stacked gated Mamba-inspired depth-wise-convolutional blocks for linear-complexity token mixing, and a dual classification plus scalar reliability head trained under a joint focal, Brier-style calibration and reliability BCE objective.

This deposit contains: the model definition and training/evaluation pipeline; the stratified five-fold cross-validation harness; temperature scaling and Brier/NLL/ECE/MCE calibration analyses; the selective-prediction (risk-coverage) evaluation of the reliability head; the raw-versus-Gaussian preprocessing ablation; a gated-block-versus-Mamba ablation scaffold; the frequency-attenuation analysis; the efficiency-profiling harness; Grad-CAM and Grad-CAM++ explainability utilities; trained per-fold checkpoints; and the derived result tables, figures and logs underlying every table and figure in the article.

Data note: the retinal fundus images themselves are third-party data and are NOT included in this deposit. They must be obtained from Kaggle (Diabetic Retinopathy 224x224 Gaussian Filtered, derived from APTOS-2019) and from ADCIS (Messidor-2) under those providers' own terms. Instructions for obtaining and preparing the data are included.
